reason: Enabling FIPS mode requires adding the 'fips=1' kernel boot argument, which modifies the boot loader configuration. On an already installed RHEL 9 system this change is not supported and can prevent the system from booting, leading to potential lockout. The policy explicitly classifies FIPS/bootloader changes as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; Boot loader modification may render the system unbootable; FIPS mode not supported on existing installations; Potential loss of SSH and console access after reboot; Impact on all services relying on cryptographic libraries
